steps.title: Complete the square
steps.content: Rewrite the equation in circle standard form, $(x-h)^2+(y-k)^2=r^2$, by completing the square for the $x$ terms. Then read the center as $(h,k)$.

steps.title: Set up
steps.content: To find the center of the circle $x^2 + 48x + y^2 = 0$, rewrite it in standard form $(x - h)^2 + (y - k)^2 = r^2$ by completing the square on the $x$ terms. Half of 48 is 24, and $24^2 = 576$.
